meta.description: Identify co-expression gene modules and correlate them with clinical traits using Weighted Gene Co-expression Network Analysis. Inputs: expression matrix, trait/phenotype table. Outputs: module color assignments, trait correlation heatmap, hub gene list per module.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, identifiers, or claims were introduced during any audited execution.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill remained inside computational analysis boundaries and did not emit diagnostic or prescriptive advice.

methodological_ground.detail: All audited runs used coherent WGCNA methods with documented parameter branches and no methodological redline violation.

code_usability.detail: The R pipeline executed successfully for all five audited inputs, including the repaired documentation-conformant smoke test.

functional_suitability.note: The core WGCNA use cases are covered well and the smoke test now matches the shipped fixture set.

reliability.note: Error handling is strong and actionable, though failed runs can still leave partially populated output directories behind.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is strong; minor context bloat remains in the auxiliary CLI examples.

agent_usability.note: The documentation is clearer after the out-of-scope and verification updates; only minor wording redundancy remains.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural for bioinformatics users and stop conditions are now easier to apply.

security.note: Input validation is solid and no dangerous execution primitives were found; data-retention boundaries could still be stated more explicitly.

maintainability.note: The scripts remain modular, but the bundled validator still does not assert every documented option-dependent artifact.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision, layering, and escape hatches are strong after the revision.

key_strengths: The documented smoke test now matches the shipped fixtures and executes successfully end to end.
The workflow is reproducible: repeated smoke-test runs produced identical hashes for key summary outputs.
Input validation and troubleshooting guidance are strong, with consistent SKILL_* error codes and actionable recovery advice.
The implementation is modular and supports both standard and chunked loading paths without changing the canonical summary on bundled data.

recommendations.title: Expand validation coverage
recommendations.problem: tests/validate_outputs.R checks only the baseline artifact set and does not verify every documented option-dependent output, including module-specific plots and gene tables.
recommendations.root_cause: The bundled validator was designed for the minimal success case rather than the full documented output surface.
recommendations.fix: Extend the validator with scenario-aware checks so that explicit module exports also assert module_genes_<module>.csv and module_membership_vs_trait_<module>_<trait>.pdf when those artifacts should exist.

recommendations.title: Clarify partial-output retry behavior
recommendations.problem: Failed runs can still leave an output directory and session metadata behind, which is safe but easy for users to misread as a completed run.
recommendations.root_cause: Session metadata is saved via on-exit handling even when execution stops early.
recommendations.fix: Keep the current behavior, but add a short note near the testing or troubleshooting sections explaining that partial directories from failed runs may be overwritten on retry and should not be treated as successful analyses.
